summary: Full E2E validation of Phase 1B Printability Report engine (backend analyzer + POST /api/printability/analyze + docked frontend panel). Existing 14/14 pytest suite passes with no regressions. Added 2 new HTTP tests covering the review-request field-level assertions (clean 20mm cube: score>=80, verdict='ready', is_watertight=True, triangle_count=12, has_flat_base=True; non-watertight cube: issue with code='non_watertight', severity='critical', fix_action='auto_clean'). Both pass. Playwright UI verified all 9 review-request UI cases: open button (T1), panel visible (T2), 'Nothing to analyze' error state (T3), close button hides panel (T4), add cube via toolbar (obj_count=1), reopen shows score number '100' + verdict 'Ready to print' + full metric strip (Triangles 12, Volume 8 cm³, BBox 20×20×20mm, Watertight Yes, Parts 1, Flat base Yes), refresh re-runs and score persists '100'. All data-testids present and functional.

issues: The score number ('100' in test) is present in the DOM (data-testid='printability-score-number' with correct text content) and passes tests, but is NOT visibly rendered inside the ring in the panel screenshot (ring displays as green with an empty center). Likely cause: double rotation conflict — outer <svg> has className '-rotate-90' (CSS), and the <text> element has BOTH className 'rotate-90' AND SVG attribute transform='rotate(90 56 56)'. The redundant/composed rotations may push the text off-canvas. Recommended fix in PrintabilityReportPanel.jsx around line 54-61: pick ONE counter-rotation (either the CSS class OR the transform attribute, not both) so the number renders upright inside the ring.

action_items: OPTIONAL / minor design polish: fix the ScoreRing double-rotation so the numeric score is visibly rendered inside the ring (see design_issues). Everything else in the review request is functionally verified working.
critical_code_review_comments: printability_service.py _check_watertight — mesh.outline() can be expensive on large meshes (walks all boundary edges); consider caching the result since analyze_trimesh may want to know the open-boundary count for the metric strip too. Non-blocking.

routes/printability.py `analyze()` calls `await file.read()` AFTER validating the extension — good (fails fast on bad ext without reading 100MB), but the 100MB size cap is only enforced AFTER the full read completes (len(content) > MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES). Starlette buffers the whole upload in memory by then. For hardening, either enforce a max_upload at ASGI/nginx layer or stream+chunk-limit. Non-blocking for MVP.

PrintabilityReportPanel.jsx line 176-178 — useEffect runs runAnalysis every time the `objects` array reference changes (because runAnalysis is memoized on `objects`). Editing a chip / moving an object rewrites the array and would re-run analysis while the panel is open. That's actually desirable (live re-analyze), but for a 500K-tri scene it could feel laggy. Consider debouncing or gating on explicit user action.

printabilityApi.js — the fetch uses credentials:'include' (cookie auth) but doesn't set Authorization header, so it relies purely on session_token cookie. Backend get_current_user also accepts Bearer token; frontend is consistent with the rest of the app. Fine.
PrintabilityReportPanel.jsx ScoreRing text rendering has redundant rotation (see design_issues) — the number is in DOM but not visually rendered.

routes/printability.py imports `printability_service` at module level without try/except — if trimesh or numpy fails to import at server boot, this route would 500 (or the server would crash on import). Since server.py mounts the router via build_printability_router, an ImportError here would surface at build time. Consider lazy imports if that risk matters.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_printability_http_extra.py (NEW — 2 HTTP tests for field-level assertions per review request)

success_rate.backend: 16/16 (100%) — 14 existing tests + 2 new HTTP tests all pass

test_credentials: Fresh session token seeded via MongoDB: user_id='TEST_ui_prt_1783305090721', session_token='TEST_st_ui_prt_1783305090721'. Also used the per-module fixture in test_printability.py + test_printability_http_extra.py which self-seeds and self-cleans.
seed_data_creation: Two TEST_ prefixed users + user_sessions rows in MongoDB — one via UI test (manual mongo insert, still present as of report time; safe to leave, unique prefix), the rest via pytest fixtures which auto-clean via yield teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Printability panel is fully wired: toolbar 'printability-open-btn' (ShieldCheck icon, orange) → Workspace sets printabilityOpen=true → PrintabilityReportPanel mounts (data-testid='printability-panel'). Panel auto-calls runAnalysis on open which (1) checks store.objects for non-locked+visible items — if none, sets err 'Nothing to analyze. Add or import a part first.' rendered via data-testid='printability-error'; (2) otherwise calls exportSceneToSTLBytes(objects) and POSTs to /api/printability/analyze with FormData(file, file_type='stl'), then renders ScoreRing + verdict badge + MetricStrip + IssueRow list. Fix buttons (data-testid='printability-fix-<code>') toast 'coming in the next update' — this is expected MVP stub, plumbing is in place. Backend endpoint requires auth; unauth → 401, bad ext → 400, empty file → 400, well-formed but unparseable → 422, real error → 500. All allowed exts: stl/obj/ply/3mf/glb/gltf. Panel Watertight tile flips red/green from metrics.is_watertight; Flat base tile from metrics.has_flat_base.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs. Only a minor visual polish item: the numeric score is present in DOM/testid but not rendered visibly inside the ring due to a redundant rotation transform combo in ScoreRing. Fix is a 1-line CSS/attribute cleanup; feature is otherwise complete and ready to ship.
